cover.hook: Meta's $13B AI data centre chose Alberta. The North has the same advantages.
cover.sub: Plus: Gordie Howe Bridge opens July 27, Thunder Bay earns $709K, a sub-$3 frontier model, and a NWO science centre hits a delay.

reelSub: Meta goes to Alberta, bridge opens July 27, and a Thunder Bay project is delayed.
storySub: Five moves from this week that affect how you compete, ship, and build in the Northwest.

items.title: Meta picks Alberta for Canada's first AI data centre
items.body: Meta broke ground July 8 on a 1GW data centre in Sturgeon County — Canada's largest AI infrastructure bet. Alberta won on cheap power and cold air. Northwestern Ontario has both.
items.tease: Next: Canada-US trade gets a second Detroit crossing.
items.reelLine: Meta's $13B AI centre lands in Alberta — the same advantages exist in the North.

items.title: Gordie Howe Bridge finally gets its opening date
items.body: The $4.4B Windsor-Detroit bridge opens July 27, announced July 10 by Canada and Michigan. A second crossing on the busiest Canada-US trade corridor means more capacity for NWO goods moving south.
items.tease: Next: Thunder Bay earns its third housing payout.
items.reelLine: Gordie Howe Bridge opens July 27 — more capacity for Ontario-US trade.

items.title: Thunder Bay hits housing targets for the third year running
items.body: Ontario delivered a final $709,280 Building Faster Fund payment to Thunder Bay on July 10. Three years, three payouts — the program is now being sunsetted. Funds go toward 2,100 new housing units by 2031.
items.tease: Next: a frontier AI model now cheaper than ever.
items.reelLine: $709K to Thunder Bay for hitting housing targets three years straight.

items.title: Grok 4.5: Opus-class coding model at $2 per million tokens
items.body: SpaceXAI launched Grok 4.5 on July 8 — Elon calls it Opus-class but cheaper and faster. At $2/$6 per million tokens, it joins a wave of capable models making AI workflows dramatically cheaper to run.
items.tease: Next: a Thunder Bay infrastructure project delayed.
items.reelLine: Grok 4.5 out July 8 — Opus-class performance, $2 per million tokens.

items.title: Thunder Bay's $80M science centre has no new timeline
items.body: Science North confirmed this week that its planned $80M waterfront science centre in Thunder Bay will take longer than expected, with no revised date set. The project carries $20M in federal funding and was headed for a spring 2027 groundbreaking.
items.reelLine: $80M Thunder Bay science centre delayed — no new start date set.

summary.title: Big infrastructure moves, a trade corridor upgrade, and a local project on hold
summary.body: This week Canada's AI infrastructure race sharpened — Meta's $13B Alberta bet signals where the build is happening — while the Gordie Howe Bridge finally got its opening date, Thunder Bay locked in a housing win, and a planned NWO science centre hit a delay with no new timeline.

captions.carousel: Meta broke ground on a $13 billion AI data centre in Alberta on July 8 — Canada's largest AI infrastructure investment and a signal of where the build is happening. The Gordie Howe Bridge has its opening date: July 27, adding a second span to the busiest Canada-US trade corridor. Ontario rewarded Thunder Bay with a final $709,280 housing payment for meeting targets three years straight. SpaceXAI's Grok 4.5 arrived at $2 per million tokens, continuing the trend toward cheaper frontier-class AI. And Science North confirmed Thunder Bay's planned $80M waterfront science centre has no revised timeline. Full breakdown in The Signal — link in bio. #ThunderBay #NorthernOntario #NorthwesternOntario #AIbusiness #NWOntario #FedNor #AIinfrastructure
captions.reel: Meta's $13B AI centre chose Alberta. Gordie Howe Bridge opens July 27. Thunder Bay earns $709K. Grok 4.5 at $2 per million tokens. And a NWO science centre is delayed. Five moves from this week. thunderbayai.com #NorthernOntario #ThunderBay #AIbusiness #NWOntario
